title: Classic Shrimp Lo Mein
instructions: 1. Boil noodles to lower end of package instructions to prevent mushiness. Drain and set aside. Meanwhile, toss shrimp with salt and sesame oil until lightly coated. 2. Mix oyster sauce, light and dark soy sauce, sugar, and chicken bouillon until combined. Thin with a splash of water, add white pepper, and adjust to taste. 3. Heat oil in wok over high heat. Stir fry or sear shrimp briefly until cooked through with colored sides. Remove and set aside. 4. Add more oil if needed, then add carrot, onion, and cabbage to wok. Stir fry until slightly softened, then add garlic to bloom until fragrant. 5. Add noodles and sauce, toss until glossy and evenly coated. Return shrimp and mix to combine. Add bean sprouts and scallions at end to keep crisp. Serve warm.

title: Crispy Chilli Beef
instructions: 1. Start the rice and make the sauce: Begin rice first so it's ready when beef finishes. Mix all sauce ingredients until smooth and fully combined. Taste and adjust as needed. 2. Prep the beef: Thinly slice beef across the grain into bite-sized pieces. In a large bowl, combine with beaten egg, salt, soy sauce, sesame oil, pepper, and MSG. Mix thoroughly until evenly coated and sticky. Toss with cornstarch spoonfuls, adding more as needed to coat most pieces. Some dry and wet spots will form, creating crispy edges when fried. 3. Fry the beef: Fill wok or pan with 1-2 inches of neutral oil. Heat to 350°F/176°C. In batches, fry pieces 3-5 minutes until crispy and cooked through. Don't overcrowd the pan. Drain on wire rack or paper towel. Repeat with remaining beef. 4. Sauté aromatics: Pour off most frying oil, leaving a thin layer. Keep heat on medium-high, toss in peppers, garlic, and ginger. Sauté about 1 minute until fragrant. 5. Add sauce and beef: Add sauce and let come to simmer and reduce. It will thicken quickly and caramelize. Return cooked beef to sauce and mix until evenly coated. 6. Plate and serve: Serve sauced beef over rice. Optionally garnish with cilantro and/or chili flakes for more heat.

title: Thai Basil Beef Stir Fry
instructions: 1. Start the jasmine rice with your preferred cooking method. Roughly chop a full bunch of basil and thinly slice the shallots into half moons. Mix the sauce ingredients until combined; the sauce should be balanced but strong. 2. Set a wok or pan over medium heat. Add oil and ground beef with a sprinkle of salt. Sear undisturbed for 1-2 minutes. Using a spatula or potato masher, break up the beef as it cooks until nicely browned and pebbly in texture. 3. Add sliced shallots and stir fry until fragrant and softened. Drizzle in most of the sauce around the edges of the wok and stir fry to incorporate. Add remaining sauce if needed. Lower heat and allow sauce to reduce and caramelize. Taste and adjust seasonings. 4. Turn off heat and stir in basil. When rice is finished, add a portion to a bowl and top with the stir fry. Serve warm.

title: Thai Chile Pork & Cabbage Stir Fry
instructions: 1. Brown the pork: Preheat a pan over medium-high heat. Add the ground pork and press it into the hot pan, making a thin even layer across the hot surface. Allow the pork to sear undisturbed until the bottom begins to brown and take on some color. Using a potato masher, fork, or spatula, break up the pork into small pebble-sized bits. 2. Add the cabbage and steam: Once the pork is mostly cooked through, add in the sliced cabbage. Stir the cabbage into the meat to ensure nothing is burning on the bottom of the pan. Cover to steam and soften the cabbage for a minute or two. Remove the cover. Sauté for another minute or two to get more browning on the meat and edges of the cabbage and evaporate any excess moisture. 3. Finish with aromatics and sauces: Push the cabbage and pork mixture to one side of the pan. Add a drizzle of oil to the open space and add in the shallot and Thai chilies to bloom for about 30 seconds before mixing everything together. Add the fish sauce to deglaze any browning from the bottom of the pan. Turn off the heat. Add a drizzle of sesame oil and a sprinkle of MSG. Taste and adjust as needed. 4. Plate and serve: Serve with a fried egg and a sprinkle of sesame seeds for garnish.

title: Ginger Beef & Broccoli
instructions: 1. Place the steak in the freezer for 1-2 hours before cooking. Meanwhile, cook some rice now if you don't have any leftover. 2. Once the steak has firmed up in the freezer, slice it as thin as possible and place it in a bowl. Grate the garlic cloves and ginger using a grater. Add them to the bowl with chili flakes, a splash of soy sauce, and half of the oil. Mix everything together with your hands. Chop the broccoli into pieces for stir-frying. 3. Pour the remaining oil into a wok and set it over high heat. Once it starts to smoke, add the seasoned beef and stir-fry for 1-2 minutes until cooked through. Remove and set aside. Add a little soy sauce and water to the wok. Scrape the fond from the bottom of the pan, then add the broccoli. Let it cook until the water evaporates, then set aside. The broccoli should still be slightly crunchy. 4. To serve, place rice on the plate and sprinkle with sesame seeds. Add the broccoli, then the beef, some pickled onions, and finally another sprinkle of chili flakes.

title: Thai Sausage Stir Fry
instructions: 1. Start cooking the rice or use some that is already made. Add all Thai sausage components to a blender, food processor, or mortar and pestle, except for the fish sauce and ground pork. Blend the ingredients until they form a paste. 2. Add the pork and fish sauce to a bowl. Mix together. Next, add the aromatic paste and mix until combined. Add 1/4 of the sausage mixture to a wok or pan on medium-high heat. Let the sausage fat render and crisp up the meat. Once browned, flip the sausage and chop it up, stir-frying for another 4-5 minutes. Cook the rest of the meat right away, or store it in the freezer for later use. 3. Add the broccolini and a pinch of salt, then stir-fry with the sausage until browned and the vegetable is softened to your liking. Taste and adjust the salt to your taste. Add a serving of rice to a bowl and top with the stir fry.

title: Chicken Pad See Ew
instructions: 1. Prep the noodles and sauce: Soak noodles to rehydrate per package directions. Mix all sauce ingredients in a container until well combined. 2. Stir fry the eggs and vegetables: Heat wok or large pan over high heat with oil. Add beaten egg, swirl around pan until fluffy with browning, chop and set aside. Add more oil, garlic, and white pepper; bloom for 30-45 seconds. Add chopped broccolini, stir fry 1-2 minutes, add a drizzle of sauce, then remove to bowl with eggs. 3. Brown the chicken: Add oil to wok, add chicken slices, brown 2 minutes per side, add sauce for color and seasoning. 4. Bring it all together: Add broccolini and egg back to wok with drained noodles. Add enough sauce to coat, stir fry 5-7 minutes until noodles soften and absorb sauce. Adjust seasoning, serve with white pepper.

title: Pork & Cabbage Gyoza
instructions: 1. Make the filling and sauce: Add chopped cabbage to a bowl and mix with salt. Let sit 10 minutes. Remove excess moisture with a towel. Add ground pork, scallion, ginger, garlic, chile, salt, and MSG. Mix vigorously until sticky and pasty. Mix together the dipping sauce and adjust to taste. 2. Fill the wrappers: Take a gyoza wrapper and add a small spoonful of filling in the center. Dip your finger in water and circle the edge to wet it. 3. Fold the gyoza: Fold the wrapper in half straight to the center. Use thumb and index finger to make 3-4 pleats on the right side of the centerfold, pressing tightly. Repeat with 3-4 pleats on the left side. Press pleats to seal. 4. Sear and steam the gyoza: For freezing, place on baking sheet in freezer for 30-60 minutes until frozen, then store. To cook, drizzle oil in a pan over medium heat. Add gyoza and sear 1-2 minutes until browned. Pour in water and cover with lid to steam. For fresh gyoza, steam 3 minutes; for frozen, 4-5 minutes. Remove lid and cook 1 more minute. Transfer to plate and serve with dipping sauce.

title: Congee with Crispy Chili Pork
instructions: 1. Sauté the congee: Grate carrot, onion, and garlic. Heat pot with neutral oil and a drizzle of sesame oil. Add vegetables and sauté 2-3 minutes until browning. Add rice and mix. Pour in stock and stir. Bring to boil, then reduce heat to simmer. 2. Simmer the congee: Simmer 20-30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until rice breaks down and liquid evaporates to porridge consistency. Prepare pork while simmering. Adjust thickness and season with salt. 3. Sauté the pork: Mince chilies, garlic, and scallion whites. Heat wok with oil over medium heat, sauté aromatics 1-2 minutes. Increase to high heat, add pork and salt, crush with potato masher into small pieces, 3-5 minutes until crispy. Add water to deglaze. Once liquid evaporates, add soy sauce and a drizzle of hoisin. Mix until coated and sticky. Adjust seasoning. 4. Serve: Ladle congee into bowl, top with spoonfuls of crispy pork, garnish with ginger, scallions, and chili crisp oil.

title: Spring Roll Framework
instructions: 1. Prep the ingredients: Cook and drain the rice noodles, and set aside. Cook or prep your protein. Gather the remaining ingredients and chop up any vegetables you are using. 2. Mix the sauce: Mix together all peanut sauce ingredients until well combined. Use sriracha, garlic, ginger, or lime juice as flavor adjusters if you want. Taste and adjust as needed. 3. Assemble the rolls: Pass a rice paper through water, and then add the ingredients of choice in layers. Carefully roll it all up. Enjoy with the peanut sauce.
